steps.title: Fastest path: graph or solve the equation
steps.content:
Type the equation into Desmos as written:
$$g(x) = (36 - 2x)(10 + 2x)$$Graph it and use the visible intercept, root, or vertex the question asks for. The requested value is $6.5$.

steps.title: Find the zeros by setting each factor to zero
steps.content:
$g(x) = (36 - 2x)(10 + 2x)$ equals zero when each factor equals zero:
  • $36 - 2x = 0 \Rightarrow x = 18$
  • $10 + 2x = 0 \Rightarrow x = -5$

steps.title: Find the vertex using the midpoint of the zeros
steps.content:
For a quadratic function, the vertex (where the maximum occurs) is located at the x-coordinate equidistant from the two zeros. Apply the midpoint formula:
$$x = \frac{18 + (-5)}{2} = \frac{13}{2} = 6.5$$
This matches the correct answer — A is correct.
